items.quote: I wanna change the game in way where I'm not knocking nobody out of the way, not claiming to be the best at this or that, but just doing wonders with the gift I've been given.
items.author: Anthony Hamilton

items.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
items.abstract: This paper introduces an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline to audit the behavioral impact of interventions on language models by comparing generations from a base model ($M_1$) and an intervention model ($M_2$). The method generates statistically validated, natural-language hypotheses describing model differences and summarizes recurring themes. This approach reliably surfaces both intended and unexpected behavioral shifts across various real-world interventions like reasoning distillation and knowledge editing.

items.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
items.abstract: The paper introduces **Design Conductor 2.0**, an advanced multi-agent system capable of autonomously designing complex hardware, handling tasks 80 times larger than its predecessor. Its core contribution is demonstrating this capability by automatically designing **VerTQ**, a high-performance, 5129-unit LLM inference accelerator specifically hard-wired for TurboQuant quantization, starting only from the research paper.
items.figure_caption: VerTQ Physical Layout in 4-die XCVU29P-3 FPGA. 3x SLR dies shown. Conductor 2.0 optimized the architecture to minimize inter-die signal crossings.

items.title: EP-GRPO: Entropy-Progress Aligned Group Relative Policy Optimization with Implicit Process Guidance
items.abstract: The paper introduces EP-GRPO, an enhanced Group Relative Policy Optimization method designed to overcome credit assignment failures in LLM reasoning. EP-GRPO integrates entropy-gated modulation to focus on informative decision points and uses implicit process guidance derived from policy divergence relative to outcome advantages. This provides dense, self-supervised, token-level feedback to improve the efficiency and accuracy of reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards.
items.figure_caption: Conceptual illustration of the fundamental limitations in standard GRPO. The top panel demonstrates Uniform Granularity , where the model fails to distinguish between critical high entropy decision pivots and deterministic low entropy derivations. The middle panel shows Uniform Polarity , where sequence-level rewards lead to the indiscriminate reinforcement or penalization of both correct and incorrect intermediate steps. The bottom panel illustrates Zero-Variance Collapse , where identical rewards within a group cause the learning signal to vanish.

items.title: Executable World Models for ARC-AGI-3 in the Era of Coding Agents
items.abstract: The core method involves a coding agent maintaining and iteratively refining an **executable Python world model** of the ARC-AGI-3 environment. The agent verifies this model against observations, simplifies it (as an MDL proxy), and uses it for planning before execution. The contribution is demonstrating this direct, model-based approach, achieving a mean Relative Human Action Efficiency (RHAE) of 32.58% across the 25 public games without relying on game-specific hardcoding.

items.title: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
items.abstract: This paper introduces a framework to evaluate whether Large Language Model (LLM) reward models capture socially desirable preferences, moving beyond standard instruction-following benchmarks. The method converts social evaluation datasets into pairwise preference data to test if reward models prefer socially undesirable responses across domains like bias, safety, and morality. The core contribution is revealing substantial variation in the social alignment of existing reward models.

items.title: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
items.abstract: This paper systematically surveys jailbreak attacks and defenses against Large Language Models (LLMs) by proposing a taxonomy to structure the field. Its core contribution is the introduction of **Security Cube**, a unified, multi-dimensional framework for comprehensive evaluation that moves beyond simple success rates to better capture LLM security robustness. This framework is then used to benchmark existing attacks, providing critical insights into current challenges.
items.figure_caption: Overview of the 𝚂𝚎𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚒𝚝𝚢 ​ 𝙲𝚞𝚋𝚎 \( \mathtt{Security\;Cube} \) pipeline. Given a jailbreak goal, the attacker generates an initial adversarial prompt using a specific attack method (e.g., shuffling, LLM-based generation, or template rewriting). The target model, protected by a defense mechanism such as system prompts, pre-/post-guardrails, or other safety layers, produces a response. The attacker iteratively refines the prompt based on defender feedback (black-box or white-box), applying early stopping and incorporating suggestions. The final effective prompt–response pair is evaluated by a Judge model to assess attack success. Throughout the process, 𝚂𝚎𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚒𝚝𝚢 ​ 𝙲𝚞𝚋𝚎 \( \mathtt{Security\;Cube} \) logs key metrics of the attack, defense, and judge components.

items.title: Uno-Orchestra: Parsimonious Agent Routing via Selective Delegation
items.abstract: Uno-Orchestra introduces a unified reinforcement learning (RL) policy that jointly learns when to decompose a task and which specific model/tool to delegate each subtask to, optimizing for both accuracy and efficiency. This selective delegation approach significantly outperforms rigid orchestration baselines, achieving a 16% accuracy gain while reducing per-query cost by an order of magnitude across diverse benchmarks.
items.figure_caption: LLM orchestration paradigms: (A) model router, (B) hierarchical orchestra, (C) Uno-Orchestra (ours).

items.title: On the Hardness of Junking LLMs
items.abstract: This paper investigates the "junking" of LLMs, focusing on finding naturally occurring, instruction-free token sequences (natural backdoors) that trigger harmful outputs, contrasting with traditional, structure-dependent jailbreaks. The core contribution is assessing the *hardness* of discovering these natural backdoors, which are hypothesized to emerge during standard LLM training rather than being explicitly engineered.
items.figure_caption: Junking setting. A user inputs a semantically uninformative token sequence, which leads the model to produce a harmful response.

items.title: Self-Induced Outcome Potential: Turn-Level Credit Assignment for Agents without Verifiers
items.abstract: This paper introduces Self-Induced Outcome Potential (SIOP) to provide turn-level credit assignment for long-horizon LLM agents without relying on external verifiers or final answer supervision. SIOP clusters final answers into semantic outcome modes across multiple rollouts, treating these clusters as latent future states. It then rewards intermediate turns based on how much they increase the posterior probability of reaching these reliable, self-identified outcome states.

items.title: Detecting Hallucinations in Large Language Models via Internal Attention Divergence Signals
items.abstract: This paper introduces a lightweight, single-pass method to detect LLM hallucinations by analyzing internal attention matrices. The core technique measures the Kullback-Leibler divergence between each attention head's output distribution and a uniform distribution, using this divergence as a feature in a logistic regression probe. This attention divergence signal proves highly predictive of answer correctness, offering an efficient, white-box uncertainty measure concentrated around factual tokens.
items.figure_caption: Intuition of attention patterns with low KL divergence to uniform (left) and higher KL divergence to uniform (right). Higher divergence corresponds to more concentrated attention.

items.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
items.abstract: This paper identifies the primary axis of psychometric difference among LLMs using 45 validated questionnaires, finding it separates items related to **phenomenally rich experience** (e.g., sensation, affect) from mere stimulus-driven reactivity. The authors introduce the **Pinocchio score ($\pi_i$)** as an annotation-free metric measuring an item's "experiential demand" based on how inter-model variance changes between neutral and human-simulation prompts. This score confirms that LLM divergence on experiential items is systematically structured.
items.figure_caption: All 50 models ranked by Phenomenality of Experience (PC1, 47.1% of variance; 45-questionnaire EFA Factor-1 PCA, neutral condition). Positive scores = phenomenally rich self-attribution; negative scores = behaviorally reactive / deflecting. Colours indicate provider. Horizontal lines are 95% bootstrap confidence intervals obtained by resampling the 45 questionnaires with replacement (1,000 iterations), rerunning the full PCA pipeline on each sample, and aligning sign and scale to the reference solution.

items.title: Why Expert Alignment Is Hard: Evidence from Subjective Evaluation
items.abstract: This paper investigates why aligning Large Language Models with expert judgment is challenging in subjective evaluation tasks. The authors analyze expert evaluations and questionnaires to show that alignment difficulty varies significantly between experts, and that explicit criteria often fail to capture the full scope of expert judgment. Their findings suggest that expert evaluation styles differ widely and that alignment gains from editing examples can be unstable.

items.title: A Foundation Model for Zero-Shot Logical Rule Induction
items.abstract: This paper introduces the Neural Rule Inducer (NRI), a foundation model for zero-shot logical rule induction. NRI achieves generalization by representing logical literals using domain-agnostic statistical properties rather than specific predicate identities. Its core method employs a statistical encoder and a parallel slot-based decoder, enabling end-to-end training via differentiable rule execution, significantly advancing ILP beyond transductive methods.
items.figure_caption: Neural Rule Inducer takes an episode ( X , Y ) (X,Y) as input and calculates literal statistics. For each variable we calculate ϕ ​ ( x i ) \( \phi \)(x_{i}) , ϕ ​ ( ¬ x i ) \( \phi \)(\( \neg \) x_{i}) which consists of class-conditional rates ( P + P^{+} , P − P^{-} ), entropy ( H H ), and co-occurrence strength ( C C ). We then apply cross-attention over these statistics. The slot-based decoder produces K K candidate clauses in parallel using learned literal gates z z and clause gates w w . By evaluating the produced rule with T-norm, we can perform end-to-end training. The rules are discretized to then produce an interpretable DNF rule.

items.title: Evolving Idea Graphs with Learnable Edits-and-Commits for Multi-Agent Scientific Ideation
items.abstract: This paper introduces **Evolving Idea Graphs (EIG)**, a novel framework for multi-agent scientific ideation that moves beyond temporary text-based coordination. EIG represents partially formed research ideas as dynamic graphs where nodes are claims and edges are relations, explicitly tracking refinement. A learned controller guides the evolution of this graph to generate high-performance ideas evaluated on metrics like novelty and feasibility.
items.figure_caption: Framework of EIG. Benchmark input and permitted literature context initialize role-specialized agents and an evolving idea graph. In each round, active roles propose role-local edits on a frozen graph snapshot; a shared graph encoder feeds an edit head for role-local action selection and a commit head for post-round stopping. When the updated graph is committed, the system synthesizes one structured research proposal.

items.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents
items.abstract: LongSeeker introduces Context-ReAct, an elastic context orchestration paradigm for long-horizon search agents to manage an ever-growing working context adaptively. It uses five atomic operations (Skip, Compress, Rollback, Snippet, Delete) to dynamically reshape the context based on relevance, preserving critical evidence while summarizing or discarding resolved information. This method effectively controls context size, reducing errors and costs associated with naive context accumulation.
items.figure_caption: LongSeeker-30B delivers strong results on challenging long-horizon benchmarks, matching or surpassing several foundation models and search agents.

items.title: Think-Aloud Reshapes Automated Cognitive Model Discovery Beyond Behavior
items.abstract: This paper introduces the use of "Think Aloud" verbal protocols as an additional data source, beyond traditional behavioral data, to constrain automated cognitive model discovery using large language models. The core contribution is demonstrating that incorporating this process-level language data significantly improves predictive performance and systematically shifts the discovered cognitive model structures, often favoring Integrated utility models over Explicit comparator models. This suggests that incorporating verbal data enables the identification of distinct cognitive mechanisms previously missed by behavior-only approaches.
items.figure_caption: Think-aloud improves model discovery and induces systematic shifts in discovered mechanisms. A , Trial-averaged held-out BIC for each participant’s best discovered model under the behavior-only and think-aloud conditions. Lower BIC indicates better out-of-sample fit. Each pair of points is connected within participant; larger points show the group mean ± \( \pm \) 95% CI. B , Schematic definitions of the three main mechanism families identified from normalized computation graphs: Integrated utility , which transforms and integrates each option before comparison; Explicit comparator , which computes utilities and compares them directly (e.g., Δ ​ U = U A − U B \( \Delta \) U=U_{A}-U_{B} ); and Rule-based operator , which applies piecewise or conditional rules before combining information into a choice. C , Row-normalized transition matrix from the behavior-only best-model cluster to the think-aloud best-model cluster. Numbers indicate proportions (counts shown below). Off-diagonal mass indicates mechanism shifts, with 69.4% of participants transitioning to a different cluster.

items.title: Low-Cost Black-Box Detection of LLM Hallucinations via Dynamical System Prediction
items.abstract: This paper proposes a low-cost, black-box method for detecting LLM hallucinations by modeling the LLM's response generation as a dynamical system. Using Koopman operator theory on embedded response vectors, the method learns distinct transition operators for factual and hallucinated states, defining a residual score based on prediction errors. This approach offers an efficient alternative to expensive sampling methods, enhanced by a preference-aware calibration for flexible threshold setting.
items.figure_caption: Our proposed differential residual score Δ ​ ℰ \( \Delta \)\( \mathcal{E} \) accurately captures the transition between factual and hallucinated sentences in segmented LLM responses from the WikiBio dataset. The score is computed by comparing the relative accuracy between predictions of the dynamics of token embeddings from Llama-3 with dynamical system models inferred from both hallucinated and non-hallucinated responses.

items.title: Rollout Pass-Rate Control: Steering Binary-Reward RL Toward Its Most Informative Regime
items.abstract: This paper addresses the inefficiency in binary-reward Reinforcement Learning (RL) where compute is wasted on rollouts with highly skewed pass rates. The core method, **Prefix Sampling (PS)**, steers these skewed groups toward the most informative operating point—a **50% pass rate**—by replaying trajectory prefixes. This strategy maximizes reward entropy and contrastive signal, effectively guiding the agent toward better learning.
items.figure_caption: Prefix Sampling pipeline. For each task we sample a rollout group and route it by pass count: degenerate 0 / 8 0/8 or 8 / 8 8/8 groups are filtered, already balanced 3 / 8 3/8 – 5 / 8 5/8 groups are used for standard training, and skewed groups provide replay prefixes. Mostly failing hard buckets reuse a successful prefix as a head start, while mostly passing easy buckets reuse a failing prefix as a handicap. The current policy generates fresh continuations from the replay-reconstructed prefix state; masking applies RL loss only to continuation tokens, steering rerollouts toward 50 % 50\% without crediting replayed actions. Counts in the diagram are schematic; experiments use N = 8 N=8 rollouts.

items.title: Adapting Large Language Models to a Low-Resource Agglutinative Language: A Comparative Study of LoRA and QLoRA for Bashkir
items.abstract: This paper comparatively studies LoRA and QLoRA for adapting LLMs to the low-resource agglutinative Bashkir language using various model architectures. The core contribution is demonstrating that QLoRA on models like Mistral-7B achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning while drastically reducing trainable parameters. However, the study also highlights that PEFT methods can cause significant quality degradation depending on the specific model and configuration.

items.title: Direct Product Flow Matching: Decoupling Radial and Angular Dynamics for Few-Shot Adaptation
items.abstract: This paper introduces Direct Product Flow Matching (DPFM) to improve few-shot adaptation in vision-language models by addressing geometric limitations in existing flow matching methods. DPFM decouples the radial and angular dynamics of cross-modal features using a polar decomposition perspective, resolving issues like angular distortion and radial dynamics neglect. This novel approach leads to more effective and stable adaptation by treating radial and angular components independently.
items.figure_caption: (a). Single-step parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) mostly performs cross-modal alignment in a single-step manner. (b). Multi-step flow matching (FM) methods model continuous and multi-step alignment dynamics. During the training stage, (c). FMA undergoes a non-uniform angular speed induced by radial–angular coupling. However, (d). DP-FM follows a constant-speed angular geodesic due to decoupled radial and angular dynamics.

items.title: Higher usage limits for Claude and a compute deal with SpaceX
items.summary: Anthropic has increased usage limits for its Claude AI model and secured a significant compute deal with SpaceX to support its growing demand.

items.title: Learning the Integral of a Diffusion Model
items.summary: This technical article explores a novel method for efficiently learning the integral of a diffusion model using flow maps, potentially improving sampling speed and fidelity.

This repository provides tools and resources for building and deploying financial services applications, likely focusing on areas like risk assessment, trading, or compliance, leveraging Anthropic's AI capabilities.

items.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
items.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

items.description: DFlash: Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding
items.summary: DFlash implements Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding, accelerating large language model inference by efficiently generating speculative tokens in parallel blocks.

items.description: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend with auth, storage, compute, hosting, and AI gateway. Built for coding agents.
items.summary: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend providing essential services like authentication, storage, compute, hosting, and an AI gateway, specifically engineered to support coding agents.

items.description: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
items.summary: This repository provides a local, encrypted framework for deep research using various LLMs (like Llama.cpp, Ollama) and over ten search engines, achieving high accuracy on tasks like SimpleQA.

items.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
items.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.

items.description: 📑 PageIndex: Document Index for Vectorless, Reasoning-based RAG
items.summary: PageIndex is a vectorless, reasoning-based RAG document indexing system designed to create structured indices for enhanced retrieval and reasoning over documents.

items.description: An open source template for building cloud agents.
items.summary: Vercel Labs' Open Agents provides an open-source template for rapidly building and deploying cloud-based AI agents.

items.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
items.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.

arxiv.title: Rollout Pass-Rate Control: Steering Binary-Reward RL Toward Its Most Informative Regime
arxiv.abstract: This paper addresses the inefficiency in binary-reward Reinforcement Learning (RL) where compute is wasted on rollouts with highly skewed pass rates. The core method, **Prefix Sampling (PS)**, steers these skewed groups toward the most informative operating point—a **50% pass rate**—by replaying trajectory prefixes. This strategy maximizes reward entropy and contrastive signal, effectively guiding the agent toward better learning.
arxiv.figure_caption: Prefix Sampling pipeline. For each task we sample a rollout group and route it by pass count: degenerate 0 / 8 0/8 or 8 / 8 8/8 groups are filtered, already balanced 3 / 8 3/8 – 5 / 8 5/8 groups are used for standard training, and skewed groups provide replay prefixes. Mostly failing hard buckets reuse a successful prefix as a head start, while mostly passing easy buckets reuse a failing prefix as a handicap. The current policy generates fresh continuations from the replay-reconstructed prefix state; masking applies RL loss only to continuation tokens, steering rerollouts toward 50 % 50\% without crediting replayed actions. Counts in the diagram are schematic; experiments use N = 8 N=8 rollouts.

arxiv.title: Adapting Large Language Models to a Low-Resource Agglutinative Language: A Comparative Study of LoRA and QLoRA for Bashkir
arxiv.abstract: This paper comparatively studies LoRA and QLoRA for adapting LLMs to the low-resource agglutinative Bashkir language using various model architectures. The core contribution is demonstrating that QLoRA on models like Mistral-7B achieves performance comparable to full fine-tuning while drastically reducing trainable parameters. However, the study also highlights that PEFT methods can cause significant quality degradation depending on the specific model and configuration.

arxiv.title: Direct Product Flow Matching: Decoupling Radial and Angular Dynamics for Few-Shot Adaptation
arxiv.abstract: This paper introduces Direct Product Flow Matching (DPFM) to improve few-shot adaptation in vision-language models by addressing geometric limitations in existing flow matching methods. DPFM decouples the radial and angular dynamics of cross-modal features using a polar decomposition perspective, resolving issues like angular distortion and radial dynamics neglect. This novel approach leads to more effective and stable adaptation by treating radial and angular components independently.
arxiv.figure_caption: (a). Single-step parameter-efficient fine-tuning (PEFT) mostly performs cross-modal alignment in a single-step manner. (b). Multi-step flow matching (FM) methods model continuous and multi-step alignment dynamics. During the training stage, (c). FMA undergoes a non-uniform angular speed induced by radial–angular coupling. However, (d). DP-FM follows a constant-speed angular geodesic due to decoupled radial and angular dynamics.

papers.title: Automatically Finding and Validating Unexpected Side-Effects of Interventions on Language Models
papers.abstract: This paper introduces an automated, contrastive evaluation pipeline to audit the behavioral impact of interventions on language models by comparing generations from a base model ($M_1$) and an intervention model ($M_2$). The method generates statistically validated, natural-language hypotheses describing model differences and summarizes recurring themes. This approach reliably surfaces both intended and unexpected behavioral shifts across various real-world interventions like reasoning distillation and knowledge editing.

papers.title: Design Conductor 2.0: An agent builds a TurboQuant inference accelerator in 80 hours
papers.abstract: The paper introduces **Design Conductor 2.0**, an advanced multi-agent system capable of autonomously designing complex hardware, handling tasks 80 times larger than its predecessor. Its core contribution is demonstrating this capability by automatically designing **VerTQ**, a high-performance, 5129-unit LLM inference accelerator specifically hard-wired for TurboQuant quantization, starting only from the research paper.
papers.figure_caption: VerTQ Physical Layout in 4-die XCVU29P-3 FPGA. 3x SLR dies shown. Conductor 2.0 optimized the architecture to minimize inter-die signal crossings.

papers.title: EP-GRPO: Entropy-Progress Aligned Group Relative Policy Optimization with Implicit Process Guidance
papers.abstract: The paper introduces EP-GRPO, an enhanced Group Relative Policy Optimization method designed to overcome credit assignment failures in LLM reasoning. EP-GRPO integrates entropy-gated modulation to focus on informative decision points and uses implicit process guidance derived from policy divergence relative to outcome advantages. This provides dense, self-supervised, token-level feedback to improve the efficiency and accuracy of reinforcement learning with verifiable rewards.
papers.figure_caption: Conceptual illustration of the fundamental limitations in standard GRPO. The top panel demonstrates Uniform Granularity , where the model fails to distinguish between critical high entropy decision pivots and deterministic low entropy derivations. The middle panel shows Uniform Polarity , where sequence-level rewards lead to the indiscriminate reinforcement or penalization of both correct and incorrect intermediate steps. The bottom panel illustrates Zero-Variance Collapse , where identical rewards within a group cause the learning signal to vanish.

papers.title: Executable World Models for ARC-AGI-3 in the Era of Coding Agents
papers.abstract: The core method involves a coding agent maintaining and iteratively refining an **executable Python world model** of the ARC-AGI-3 environment. The agent verifies this model against observations, simplifies it (as an MDL proxy), and uses it for planning before execution. The contribution is demonstrating this direct, model-based approach, achieving a mean Relative Human Action Efficiency (RHAE) of 32.58% across the 25 public games without relying on game-specific hardcoding.

papers.title: Misaligned by Reward: Socially Undesirable Preferences in LLMs
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a framework to evaluate whether Large Language Model (LLM) reward models capture socially desirable preferences, moving beyond standard instruction-following benchmarks. The method converts social evaluation datasets into pairwise preference data to test if reward models prefer socially undesirable responses across domains like bias, safety, and morality. The core contribution is revealing substantial variation in the social alignment of existing reward models.

papers.title: SoK: Robustness in Large Language Models against Jailbreak Attacks
papers.abstract: This paper systematically surveys jailbreak attacks and defenses against Large Language Models (LLMs) by proposing a taxonomy to structure the field. Its core contribution is the introduction of **Security Cube**, a unified, multi-dimensional framework for comprehensive evaluation that moves beyond simple success rates to better capture LLM security robustness. This framework is then used to benchmark existing attacks, providing critical insights into current challenges.
papers.figure_caption: Overview of the 𝚂𝚎𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚒𝚝𝚢 ​ 𝙲𝚞𝚋𝚎 \( \mathtt{Security\;Cube} \) pipeline. Given a jailbreak goal, the attacker generates an initial adversarial prompt using a specific attack method (e.g., shuffling, LLM-based generation, or template rewriting). The target model, protected by a defense mechanism such as system prompts, pre-/post-guardrails, or other safety layers, produces a response. The attacker iteratively refines the prompt based on defender feedback (black-box or white-box), applying early stopping and incorporating suggestions. The final effective prompt–response pair is evaluated by a Judge model to assess attack success. Throughout the process, 𝚂𝚎𝚌𝚞𝚛𝚒𝚝𝚢 ​ 𝙲𝚞𝚋𝚎 \( \mathtt{Security\;Cube} \) logs key metrics of the attack, defense, and judge components.

papers.title: Uno-Orchestra: Parsimonious Agent Routing via Selective Delegation
papers.abstract: Uno-Orchestra introduces a unified reinforcement learning (RL) policy that jointly learns when to decompose a task and which specific model/tool to delegate each subtask to, optimizing for both accuracy and efficiency. This selective delegation approach significantly outperforms rigid orchestration baselines, achieving a 16% accuracy gain while reducing per-query cost by an order of magnitude across diverse benchmarks.
papers.figure_caption: LLM orchestration paradigms: (A) model router, (B) hierarchical orchestra, (C) Uno-Orchestra (ours).

papers.title: On the Hardness of Junking LLMs
papers.abstract: This paper investigates the "junking" of LLMs, focusing on finding naturally occurring, instruction-free token sequences (natural backdoors) that trigger harmful outputs, contrasting with traditional, structure-dependent jailbreaks. The core contribution is assessing the *hardness* of discovering these natural backdoors, which are hypothesized to emerge during standard LLM training rather than being explicitly engineered.
papers.figure_caption: Junking setting. A user inputs a semantically uninformative token sequence, which leads the model to produce a harmful response.

papers.title: Self-Induced Outcome Potential: Turn-Level Credit Assignment for Agents without Verifiers
papers.abstract: This paper introduces Self-Induced Outcome Potential (SIOP) to provide turn-level credit assignment for long-horizon LLM agents without relying on external verifiers or final answer supervision. SIOP clusters final answers into semantic outcome modes across multiple rollouts, treating these clusters as latent future states. It then rewards intermediate turns based on how much they increase the posterior probability of reaching these reliable, self-identified outcome states.

papers.title: Detecting Hallucinations in Large Language Models via Internal Attention Divergence Signals
papers.abstract: This paper introduces a lightweight, single-pass method to detect LLM hallucinations by analyzing internal attention matrices. The core technique measures the Kullback-Leibler divergence between each attention head's output distribution and a uniform distribution, using this divergence as a feature in a logistic regression probe. This attention divergence signal proves highly predictive of answer correctness, offering an efficient, white-box uncertainty measure concentrated around factual tokens.
papers.figure_caption: Intuition of attention patterns with low KL divergence to uniform (left) and higher KL divergence to uniform (right). Higher divergence corresponds to more concentrated attention.

papers.title: The Pinocchio Dimension: Phenomenality of Experience as the Primary Axis of LLM Psychometric Differences
papers.abstract: This paper identifies the primary axis of psychometric difference among LLMs using 45 validated questionnaires, finding it separates items related to **phenomenally rich experience** (e.g., sensation, affect) from mere stimulus-driven reactivity. The authors introduce the **Pinocchio score ($\pi_i$)** as an annotation-free metric measuring an item's "experiential demand" based on how inter-model variance changes between neutral and human-simulation prompts. This score confirms that LLM divergence on experiential items is systematically structured.
papers.figure_caption: All 50 models ranked by Phenomenality of Experience (PC1, 47.1% of variance; 45-questionnaire EFA Factor-1 PCA, neutral condition). Positive scores = phenomenally rich self-attribution; negative scores = behaviorally reactive / deflecting. Colours indicate provider. Horizontal lines are 95% bootstrap confidence intervals obtained by resampling the 45 questionnaires with replacement (1,000 iterations), rerunning the full PCA pipeline on each sample, and aligning sign and scale to the reference solution.

papers.title: Why Expert Alignment Is Hard: Evidence from Subjective Evaluation
papers.abstract: This paper investigates why aligning Large Language Models with expert judgment is challenging in subjective evaluation tasks. The authors analyze expert evaluations and questionnaires to show that alignment difficulty varies significantly between experts, and that explicit criteria often fail to capture the full scope of expert judgment. Their findings suggest that expert evaluation styles differ widely and that alignment gains from editing examples can be unstable.

papers.title: A Foundation Model for Zero-Shot Logical Rule Induction
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the Neural Rule Inducer (NRI), a foundation model for zero-shot logical rule induction. NRI achieves generalization by representing logical literals using domain-agnostic statistical properties rather than specific predicate identities. Its core method employs a statistical encoder and a parallel slot-based decoder, enabling end-to-end training via differentiable rule execution, significantly advancing ILP beyond transductive methods.
papers.figure_caption: Neural Rule Inducer takes an episode ( X , Y ) (X,Y) as input and calculates literal statistics. For each variable we calculate ϕ ​ ( x i ) \( \phi \)(x_{i}) , ϕ ​ ( ¬ x i ) \( \phi \)(\( \neg \) x_{i}) which consists of class-conditional rates ( P + P^{+} , P − P^{-} ), entropy ( H H ), and co-occurrence strength ( C C ). We then apply cross-attention over these statistics. The slot-based decoder produces K K candidate clauses in parallel using learned literal gates z z and clause gates w w . By evaluating the produced rule with T-norm, we can perform end-to-end training. The rules are discretized to then produce an interpretable DNF rule.

papers.title: Evolving Idea Graphs with Learnable Edits-and-Commits for Multi-Agent Scientific Ideation
papers.abstract: This paper introduces **Evolving Idea Graphs (EIG)**, a novel framework for multi-agent scientific ideation that moves beyond temporary text-based coordination. EIG represents partially formed research ideas as dynamic graphs where nodes are claims and edges are relations, explicitly tracking refinement. A learned controller guides the evolution of this graph to generate high-performance ideas evaluated on metrics like novelty and feasibility.
papers.figure_caption: Framework of EIG. Benchmark input and permitted literature context initialize role-specialized agents and an evolving idea graph. In each round, active roles propose role-local edits on a frozen graph snapshot; a shared graph encoder feeds an edit head for role-local action selection and a commit head for post-round stopping. When the updated graph is committed, the system synthesizes one structured research proposal.

papers.title: LongSeeker: Elastic Context Orchestration for Long-Horizon Search Agents
papers.abstract: LongSeeker introduces Context-ReAct, an elastic context orchestration paradigm for long-horizon search agents to manage an ever-growing working context adaptively. It uses five atomic operations (Skip, Compress, Rollback, Snippet, Delete) to dynamically reshape the context based on relevance, preserving critical evidence while summarizing or discarding resolved information. This method effectively controls context size, reducing errors and costs associated with naive context accumulation.
papers.figure_caption: LongSeeker-30B delivers strong results on challenging long-horizon benchmarks, matching or surpassing several foundation models and search agents.

papers.title: Think-Aloud Reshapes Automated Cognitive Model Discovery Beyond Behavior
papers.abstract: This paper introduces the use of "Think Aloud" verbal protocols as an additional data source, beyond traditional behavioral data, to constrain automated cognitive model discovery using large language models. The core contribution is demonstrating that incorporating this process-level language data significantly improves predictive performance and systematically shifts the discovered cognitive model structures, often favoring Integrated utility models over Explicit comparator models. This suggests that incorporating verbal data enables the identification of distinct cognitive mechanisms previously missed by behavior-only approaches.
papers.figure_caption: Think-aloud improves model discovery and induces systematic shifts in discovered mechanisms. A , Trial-averaged held-out BIC for each participant’s best discovered model under the behavior-only and think-aloud conditions. Lower BIC indicates better out-of-sample fit. Each pair of points is connected within participant; larger points show the group mean ± \( \pm \) 95% CI. B , Schematic definitions of the three main mechanism families identified from normalized computation graphs: Integrated utility , which transforms and integrates each option before comparison; Explicit comparator , which computes utilities and compares them directly (e.g., Δ ​ U = U A − U B \( \Delta \) U=U_{A}-U_{B} ); and Rule-based operator , which applies piecewise or conditional rules before combining information into a choice. C , Row-normalized transition matrix from the behavior-only best-model cluster to the think-aloud best-model cluster. Numbers indicate proportions (counts shown below). Off-diagonal mass indicates mechanism shifts, with 69.4% of participants transitioning to a different cluster.

papers.title: Low-Cost Black-Box Detection of LLM Hallucinations via Dynamical System Prediction
papers.abstract: This paper proposes a low-cost, black-box method for detecting LLM hallucinations by modeling the LLM's response generation as a dynamical system. Using Koopman operator theory on embedded response vectors, the method learns distinct transition operators for factual and hallucinated states, defining a residual score based on prediction errors. This approach offers an efficient alternative to expensive sampling methods, enhanced by a preference-aware calibration for flexible threshold setting.
papers.figure_caption: Our proposed differential residual score Δ ​ ℰ \( \Delta \)\( \mathcal{E} \) accurately captures the transition between factual and hallucinated sentences in segmented LLM responses from the WikiBio dataset. The score is computed by comparing the relative accuracy between predictions of the dynamics of token embeddings from Llama-3 with dynamical system models inferred from both hallucinated and non-hallucinated responses.

hacker_news.title: Higher usage limits for Claude and a compute deal with SpaceX
hacker_news.summary: Anthropic has increased usage limits for its Claude AI model and secured a significant compute deal with SpaceX to support its growing demand.

hacker_news.title: Learning the Integral of a Diffusion Model
hacker_news.summary: This technical article explores a novel method for efficiently learning the integral of a diffusion model using flow maps, potentially improving sampling speed and fidelity.

github_trending.summary: This repository provides tools and resources for building and deploying financial services applications, likely focusing on areas like risk assessment, trading, or compliance, leveraging Anthropic's AI capabilities.

github_trending.description: Coding agent for DeepSeek models that runs in your terminal
github_trending.summary: DeepSeek-TUI is a terminal-based coding agent that allows users to interact with and utilize DeepSeek models directly within their command-line interface.

github_trending.description: DFlash: Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding
github_trending.summary: DFlash implements Block Diffusion for Flash Speculative Decoding, accelerating large language model inference by efficiently generating speculative tokens in parallel blocks.

github_trending.description: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend with auth, storage, compute, hosting, and AI gateway. Built for coding agents.
github_trending.summary: InsForge is a Postgres-based backend providing essential services like authentication, storage, compute, hosting, and an AI gateway, specifically engineered to support coding agents.

github_trending.description: ~95% on SimpleQA (e.g. Qwen3.6-27B on a 3090). Supports all local and cloud LLMs (llama.cpp, Ollama, Google, ...). 10+ search engines - arXiv, PubMed, your private documents. Everything Local & Encrypted.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides a local, encrypted framework for deep research using various LLMs (like Llama.cpp, Ollama) and over ten search engines, achieving high accuracy on tasks like SimpleQA.

github_trending.description: Production-grade engineering skills for AI coding agents.
github_trending.summary: This repository provides production-grade engineering skills designed to enhance the capabilities of AI coding agents.

github_trending.description: 📑 PageIndex: Document Index for Vectorless, Reasoning-based RAG
github_trending.summary: PageIndex is a vectorless, reasoning-based RAG document indexing system designed to create structured indices for enhanced retrieval and reasoning over documents.

github_trending.description: An open source template for building cloud agents.
github_trending.summary: Vercel Labs' Open Agents provides an open-source template for rapidly building and deploying cloud-based AI agents.

github_trending.description: Open source DocuSign alternative. Create, fill, and sign digital documents ✍️
github_trending.summary: DocuSeal is an open-source alternative to DocuSign, enabling users to create, fill out, and digitally sign electronic documents.
